hypothesis: If a convolutional neural network (EfficientNet-B0) is pretrained on a data-rich additive-free SEM dataset and then fine-tuned on a small additive-induced SEM dataset, it will achieve higher class-wise accuracy and F1 scores for identifying NCM composition and aging state than models trained from scratch or without such staged transfer learning, because the pretrained model captures transferable morphological features (e.g., particle cracking and surface boundaries).
